records.ety: [L. cliens, -emtis, for cluens, one who hears (in relation to his protector), a client, fr. L. cluere to be named or called; akin to Gr. <?/ to hear, Skr. çry, and E. loud: cf. F. client. See Loud.]

defs: A citizen who put himself under the protection of a man of distinction and influence, who was called his patron.
A dependent; one under the protection of another.
quotes: I do think they are your friends and clients, And fearful to disturb you.

defs: One who consults a legal adviser, or submits his cause to his management.
